note: PROTOTYPE SAMPLE DATA. Curator names are placeholders; the 'why' blurbs are illustrative copy, not real curator statements. Picks here are deliberately lesser-known works (deep-cut, off-the-headline choices) to show the concept beyond marquee names; images are placeholder tiles (thumb: null).

stories.title: Five to cross the park for
stories.intro: I spend my days with American painting at the de Young, so my afternoons off are for the Legion, the European rooms that first made me want this job. Not the postcards, the works most visitors walk straight past. Here are five I'd send anyone across Golden Gate Park to find.

picks.title: Portrait of a Woman with a Lapdog
picks.why: Everyone stops at the big French rooms and misses her. Look at the dog's eyes, then the lace. Largillière painted fur and fabric like he was showing off, and he was.

picks.title: Study of Drapery
picks.why: A works-on-paper drawer holds things the walls never will. Just folds of cloth, chalk on blue paper, done to solve one problem in a painting we may never find. Pure looking.

picks.title: Covered Tureen
picks.why: People think ceramics are the boring bit between the paintings. Get close to the glaze on this and tell me a Rothko does more with a single colour.

picks.title: The Penitent Magdalen
picks.artist: Follower of Georges de La Tour
picks.why: Not the famous one, a follower's version, and better for it. One candle doing all the work. I'd trade a wall of masterpieces for this much dark.

picks.title: Medal of a Renaissance Prince
picks.why: A bronze you could hide in your palm, in a case most people don't even see. Portraiture before portraits went big. Somebody's whole face, cast to last five centuries.

stories.title: What I go to the de Young for
stories.intro: My galleries are all gilt frames and old varnish, which I love. But when I need reminding what art does now, I go to the de Young, and I skip the greatest hits. Five works that keep pulling me back across the park, none of them on the map by the door.

picks.title: Basket with Handle
picks.why: A European painting curator standing speechless in front of a basket. The stitch count on this is finer than any brushwork in my building. Nobody signs it, everybody should know it.

picks.title: Quilt (Log Cabin variation)
picks.why: Geometry a hundred years before the painters got there, made on a kitchen table. I bring my abstraction students here and don't tell them what it is until they've stared.

picks.title: Storage Jar
picks.why: A working pot, not a museum piece pretending. The repair line where it cracked and was mended is the most honest thing in the gallery.

picks.title: Landscape near Sonoma
picks.why: A local painter the crowds skip on their way to the big names. Grey-green California light nobody paints anymore because nobody sits still long enough to see it.

picks.title: Carved Feast Bowl
picks.why: Tucked in the Arts of the Pacific rooms most people run through. One piece of wood, one animal form, no wasted cut. I think about it when I'm looking at Bernini.
